mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes-AMG A45 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M133 engine typically costs £3,500–£7,500 supply only; a reconditioned M139 costs £5,000–£12,000. Used units are available from £2,000–£4,500. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500. W176 A45 engine replacement costs are 'into five figures'. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your A45.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes-AMG A45 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The A45's engines are highly-strung performance units — they produce over 190 hp per litre. Reliability depends entirely on maintenance history. Known issues include timing chain stretch, HPFP failure, turbo oil feed problems (early 2014 cars), and carbon build-up (M139). With 10,000-mile service intervals and 98/99 RON fuel, they can be reliable.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes-AMG A45?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain stretch and guide wear (the most critical issue), high-pressure fuel pump failure (power loss under load), turbocharger oil feed issues (early 2014 cars — subject to recall), carbon build-up on intake valves (M139), cooling system leaks (water pump and thermostat housing), and 7-speed DCT gearbox issues.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes-AMG A45 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: A45 values remain strong — a well-kept W176 is often worth £12,000–£25,000, while W177 cars fetch £25,000–£45,000+. A £3,500–£12,000 reconditioned engine is 15–40% of the vehicle's value — apply the Mercedes 70% scrap threshold. If the car is a cherished example, replacement is often worthwhile.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes-AMG A45 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. W176 A45 (2013–2018) uses M133; W177 A45 (2019+) uses M139. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the M133/M139 timing chain, or does the engine need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: The timing chain and tensioner can be replaced preventatively — they are wear items. Replacement costs £1,500–£3,500. If the chain fails and jumps timing, the engine is typically destroyed. A preventative tensioner replacement is far cheaper than a full engine replacement (£3,500+). At the first sign of rattling from the front of the engine, book an inspection immediately.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current A45 AMG market. W176 M133 engines sit at the lower end of the range; W177 M139 engines command a premium. W176 A45 engine replacement costs are "into five figures". Reconditioned M139 units are significantly more expensive due to their complexity and hand-built nature.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an A45 AMG engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). The transverse layout and complex turbo/ancillary packaging make it a specialist job.

problems.title: Timing Chain Stretch and Guide Wear (M133 and M139)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start, metallic ticking from the front of the engine, delayed starting, engine warning light, cam timing errors, "check engine" light
problems.replacementCost: £3,500–£7,500 (if chain jumps and causes valve interference)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if the timing chain fails, the engine is typically destroyed

problems.recommendation: The M133 and M139 engines are highly strung, producing over 190 hp per litre. This puts immense strain on the timing chain and tensioner system. One W176 A45 owner reported a timing chain that had "stretched and guides worn, cams misalignment and high pressure petrol pump faulty" — the whole cost was "equating to replacement engine". If you hear rattling on cold start, book an inspection immediately. Preventative timing chain replacement is far cheaper than a full engine replacement. Regular oil changes (every 10,000 miles) are essential.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide

problems.title: High-Pressure Fuel Pump (HPFP) Failure (M133 and M139)
problems.symptoms: Sudden loss of power under load (uphill or overtaking), check engine light, normal operation after restart, rough idle, over-fuelling
problems.recommendation: High-pressure fuel pump failure is a well-documented issue on both M133 and M139 engines. One owner had their dealership replace both low-pressure and high-pressure pumps, only for the new high-pressure pump to fail again. Symptoms include sudden power loss under load and a check engine light that clears after a restart. If you experience any hesitation or power loss, have the fuel system inspected. Using high-quality fuel (98/99 RON) is essential — the A45 "runs like a pig on 95".

failureLink.label: Read the full fuel pump failure guide

problems.title: Turbocharger Oil Feed Issues (Early M133 — 2014)
problems.symptoms: Whistling or grinding noise from the turbo, loss of power, excessive smoke, turbo wear

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: early 2014 A45s suffered from oil feed issues to the turbocharger

problems.recommendation: Early 2014 A45s suffered from a design flaw where the turbocharger lacked an anti-surge valve. Pressure could only escape through the turbo impeller, causing it to wear. Mercedes issued a recall to address this, so check if the work has been done. If you notice any whistling, grinding or loss of power from the turbo, have it inspected immediately. A failing turbo can send debris through the engine, destroying it.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: A45 AMG — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Labour-intensive but far cheaper than a full engine replacement
If chain jumps and valves hit pistons, replacement is the only option
Check if recall has been completed
Walnut blasting — well-understood by AMG specialists
Water pump and thermostat housing are known failure points

buyingChecks.title: Buying This A45 AMG? Check These First
buyIf: * 2016+ facelift W176 — later cars have improved turbo components and revised gearbox ratios
* W177 with comprehensive warranty — M139 engines are complex and expensive to repair
* Evidence of 10,000-mile service intervals — essential for these highly-strung engines
* Turbo recall work completed on early 2014 cars — check the service history
* Full service history with correct 98/99 RON fuel used
avoidIf: * Any rattling from the front of the engine — timing chain wear is likely
* Early 2014 car with no proof of turbo recall work — oil feed issues are a known risk
* Evidence of repeated HPFP failures — suggests a deeper fuel system issue
* Poor service history — these engines are unforgiving of long oil change intervals
* Any "check engine" light without a clear diagnosis

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The A45 AMG is a genuine performance icon — the world's most powerful production four-cylinder in its day, and still one of the most exciting hot hatches ever built. But these are highly-strung engines that demand meticulous maintenance. Timing chain wear, HPFP failures, and turbo oil feed issues (on early cars) are well-documented. Buy on history, maintain religiously with 10,000-mile service intervals, and use 98/99 RON fuel. Skip the maintenance and it will punish you.

cta.label: → Compare A45 AMG engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes-AMG A45 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Cold Start — Listen for rattles from the front of the engine (timing chain tensioner warning)
2. Service History — including which generation (W176 or W177) and evidence of 10,000-mile service intervals
3. Turbo Recall (2014) — Has the turbo oil feed recall been completed?
4. Fuel System — Any history of power loss under load or HPFP replacement?
5. Coolant Level — Any unexplained loss? (water pump or thermostat housing warning)
6. Gearbox — Any crunching in reverse or jerky downshifts? (7-speed DCT can be problematic)
7. Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
8. Fuel Type — Has the car been run on 98/99 RON? (95 RON causes poor running)
